response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and genuine breadth across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but the role's primary requirement is deep, production-grade C++ development for industrial automation platforms. His C++ exposure is minimal and dated — a six-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — while his skill list omits C++ entirely. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems work in C++, message-broker or MQTT experience, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle knowledge, or industrial-protocol familiarity (OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), which are central to this position.
strengths: Event-driven architecture experience through GISCollective and Sociomantic micro-services work aligns with the message-broker and distributed-systems aspects of the role
Node.js and React proficiency matches the advantageous skills list, and low-latency/high-throughput work at Dunnhumby demonstrates performance awareness
Consistent agile, TDD, and CI/CD practices across multiple employers show strong engineering discipline and quality mindset
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from the candidate's active skill set — the only relevant exposure is a 6-month role in 2014-2015 and a university thesis project, far below the 8-12 years of hands-on C++ development required
No evidence of secure development lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or any industrial automation or IoT protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No multi-threading, concurrent programming, or networking work documented in C++ context; concurrent/async experience exists only in Node.js and D-language environments
